title: Fermented Flaky Korean Oreo Flatbread
description: A quick weeknight dessert that blends the rich sweetness of Oreo cream with the punch of Korean chili paste, fermented for depth and folded for buttery flakiness.
instructions: In a medium bowl, combine crushed Oreo crumbs with melted butter, honey, gochujang, and sesame oil. Mix until crumbs are fully coated.
Whisk together milk, egg, sugar, and yeast until well blended and slightly frothy. Let sit for 5 minutes to activate the yeast.
Pour the wet mixture into the Oreo mixture and stir until combined.
Add flour and a pinch of salt; knead until a smooth dough forms, about 3–4 minutes.
Transfer dough to a lightly floured surface and knead for 2 minutes until elastic.
Place dough in a greased bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let rise at room temperature for 45 minutes or until doubled in size.
Punch the dough down, roll into a 12×8‑inch rectangle, fold once, and roll again to create layers.
Heat a non‑stick skillet over medium heat and cook the flatbread 3–4 minutes per side until golden and crisp.
Slice the flatbread into squares, sprinkle with chopped scallions if desired, and serve warm.
